summary: Tested all newly added routes and ComplianceLibraryPage coral color fixes. Root cause: main agent added React Router routes in App.js, but this is a Next.js app with file-based routing. The Next.js page files for /products/dq-file-builder, /products/safety-audit-prep, /products/bundle, and /standards/safety-audit-prep were missing. Created the 4 missing page files. All pages now return 200.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js imports usePathname from 'next/navigation' and uses BrowserRouter + React Router — this is dead code in a Next.js app. The React Router route definitions in App.js are ignored; Next.js file-based routing is what actually handles routing. Consider removing the React Router setup from App.js entirely to avoid confusion.
App.js still present with BrowserRouter wrapping — it is unclear if it's used at all in the Next.js context. The _app.jsx in pages/ likely wraps the app instead.

pages_verified./products/dq-file-builder: 200 - PASS. Coral label LP-PKT-DQ visible, coral border bar below H1, 13 coral elements
pages_verified./products/bundle: 200 - PASS. BundlePage loads with LP-BDL-001 coral label and coral bar
pages_verified./compliance-library: 200 - PASS. Coral LP-SYS-LIBRARY label, coral section labels (LP-STD-001, LP-BDL-001, domain labels). Gold still on buy buttons/prices.

rca_of_issue: Main agent added routes in App.js (React Router) but the app is Next.js — routes in App.js are ignored. Next.js uses file-based routing under /pages/. The fix was to create the 4 missing .jsx files in the correct pages/ subdirectories.
context_for_next_testing_agent: This is a Next.js app. Routing is file-based under /app/frontend/pages/. App.js with React Router is a legacy artifact. New product/standard pages need corresponding files in pages/ directory. All tested routes now return 200.
